findings.title: Design and Implement Usage-Based Billing Engine
findings.description: Migrate from flat monthly pricing to consumption-based pricing where customers pay per metric ingested and per dashboard query. This requires a metering pipeline that tracks usage in real time and a rating engine to apply pricing rules.
assumptions: The current flat-rate pricing model is not sustainable.
Customers will respond positively to a consumption-based model.

evidence.excerpt: Design and implement usage-based billing engine

recommendations: Ensure integration with Stripe for invoicing.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the new billing engine is implemented, WHEN a customer queries their usage, THEN the system should accurately reflect their consumption and calculate the corresponding charges.

findings.title: Build Pricing Calculator and Cost Estimator
findings.description: Develop an in-app pricing calculator that estimates monthly costs based on current usage patterns and a predictive cost dashboard showing projected spend for the next billing cycle.
assumptions: Customers need clarity on their expected costs.

evidence.excerpt: Build pricing calculator and cost estimator for customers

recommendations: Include a user-friendly interface for the calculator.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the pricing calculator is available, WHEN a customer inputs their usage data, THEN the calculator should provide an accurate cost estimate for the next billing cycle.

findings.title: Implement Usage Alerts and Spending Caps
findings.description: Create configurable spending alerts (50%, 75%, 90%, 100% of budget) and optional hard spending caps that pause non-critical data ingestion when exceeded.
assumptions: Customers want to manage their budgets effectively.

evidence.excerpt: Implement usage alerts and spending caps

recommendations: Ensure alerts are sent via email and in-app notifications.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the spending caps are set, WHEN a customer's usage reaches the defined thresholds, THEN the system should trigger alerts and pause non-critical data ingestion.

findings.title: Grandfather Existing Customers with 12-Month Price Lock
findings.description: Develop a grandfathering system that tracks customer cohorts, applies correct pricing tiers, and automatically transitions customers after the lock period with 60-day advance notice.
assumptions: Existing customers need to feel secure during the transition.

evidence.excerpt: Grandfather existing customers with 12-month price lock

recommendations: Communicate the grandfathering policy clearly to all affected customers.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the grandfathering system is in place, WHEN a customer is identified as an existing customer, THEN they should receive the correct pricing tier for 12 months.

findings.title: Customer Churn Risk Analysis for Pricing Migration
findings.description: Create a model that identifies which customer segments will see cost increases vs. decreases under the new pricing, flagging accounts where projected spend increases >30% for proactive outreach.
assumptions: Understanding customer segments is crucial for managing churn.

evidence.excerpt: Customer churn risk analysis for pricing migration

recommendations: Use the model to guide customer outreach efforts.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the churn risk model is developed, WHEN customer data is analyzed, THEN the system should flag accounts with projected spend increases >30%.

findings.title: Conduct Pricing Sensitivity Testing
findings.description: Run experiments to test customer sensitivity to different pricing models within the consumption-based framework to understand optimal pricing strategies.
assumptions: Testing pricing sensitivity will provide valuable insights.

evidence.excerpt: Conduct experiments to test customer sensitivity to different pricing models

recommendations: Analyze results to refine pricing strategies.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the pricing sensitivity tests are conducted, WHEN customer feedback is collected, THEN the results should inform future pricing strategies.

findings.title: Develop Predictive Cost Dashboard
findings.description: Create a dashboard that shows projected spend for the next billing cycle based on current usage patterns.
assumptions: Customers need to visualize their future costs.

evidence.excerpt: Build an in-app pricing calculator and predictive cost dashboard

recommendations: Ensure the dashboard is user-friendly and visually appealing.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the predictive cost dashboard is implemented, WHEN a customer views their usage data, THEN the dashboard should accurately display projected costs for the next billing cycle.

findings.title: Implement Customer Communication Plan for Migration
findings.description: Develop a communication plan that outlines how customers will be informed about the transition to usage-based pricing, including FAQs and support resources.
assumptions: Clear communication will reduce customer anxiety during the transition.
recommendations: Include a dedicated support channel for customer inquiries.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the communication plan is developed, WHEN customers are notified about the transition, THEN they should have access to all necessary information and support resources.
metadata.story_title: Develop Customer Communication Plan for Migration

findings.title: Create User Feedback Mechanism
findings.description: Implement a feedback mechanism to collect user insights on the new pricing model and features, allowing for continuous improvement.
assumptions: User feedback will help refine the product.

evidence.excerpt: Build pricing calculator and cost estimator for customers

recommendations: Analyze feedback regularly to inform product updates.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the feedback mechanism is in place, WHEN users provide feedback, THEN the system should collect and categorize feedback for analysis.

findings.title: Monitor Projected Churn Increase Percentage
findings.description: Establish a monitoring system for the Projected Churn Increase Percentage to ensure it remains below a critical threshold during the transition.
assumptions: Monitoring churn will help mitigate risks.

evidence.excerpt: Projected Churn Increase Percentage is a guardrail metric

recommendations: Set up alerts for when thresholds are approached.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the monitoring system is established, WHEN churn metrics are analyzed, THEN alerts should trigger if projected churn exceeds the defined threshold.

findings.title: Enhance Customer Segmentation Data
findings.description: Address the gap in customer segmentation data to better understand how different customer tiers will react to the new pricing model.
assumptions: Detailed segmentation will improve targeted communication.

evidence.excerpt: There is a gap in the data regarding detailed customer segmentation

recommendations: Conduct surveys or interviews to gather segmentation data.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the customer segmentation data is enhanced, WHEN customer data is analyzed, THEN the system should provide insights into customer reactions to pricing changes.

summary: The analysis has resulted in a comprehensive set of requirements aimed at addressing customer needs during the transition to usage-based pricing. Key areas include the implementation of a billing engine, cost estimation tools, spending controls, and customer communication strategies. The requirements are structured into epics and stories with clear acceptance criteria and dependencies to ensure effective delivery.
